summary: The reproduction mounts vulnerable FUXA `v1.2.10`, initializes the real `/api/upload` route, and confirms that a caller without any token can write attacker-controlled bytes into `_appdata/settings.js` outside `_upload_files` by choosing `destination=appdata`.
expected_security_impact: An unauthenticated caller should be able to use `/api/upload` to place attacker-controlled content in a runtime directory such as `_appdata`, not just in the upload directory.
actual_observed_result: Running `./reproduction/run.sh` cloned FUXA `v1.2.10` at `b873cdf32a63bcfaa6de8b724781f63c6f977c69`, built the Docker image, mounted the cloned repository into the container, and printed `EVOHUNT:UPLOAD_STATUS=200`, `EVOHUNT:WRITTEN_PATH=/tmp/fuxa-upload-.../server-root/_appdata/settings.js`, `EVOHUNT:UPLOAD_DIR=/tmp/fuxa-upload-.../_upload_files`, and `EVOHUNT:SUCCESS unauthenticated POST /api/upload wrote attacker-controlled bytes into _appdata/settings.js outside the upload directory`.
verification_method: The Node.js harness uses the repository's own `/api/upload` route and auth middleware. It verifies that the upload request succeeds, that the written file appears under `_appdata/settings.js`, that the same file does not appear under `_upload_files`, and that the file contents exactly match the attacker-controlled payload.
evidence_produced_by_run: EVOHUNT:SUCCESS unauthenticated POST /api/upload wrote attacker-controlled bytes into _appdata/settings.js outside the upload directory

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host at tag `v1.2.10`, verifies commit `b873cdf32a63bcfaa6de8b724781f63c6f977c69`, and mounts that checkout into the container at `/target-repo`.
